name: Manual Scenario, Suspect Perspective
aiPoliceChat: Dispatch: Suspect movement reported near the perimeter.
Officer AI: Keep hands visible and follow commands.
Officer AI: Backup requested for safe containment.
imagePrompt: Generate a perimeter checkpoint scene with officers and one suspect path.

parts.prompt: What should police do first in this situation?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Table of Contents Table of Contents Introduction Learning Outcomes Icons in This Manual Module One: Introduction to the Sec

choices.text: Report location and direction before isolated pursuit.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. Communication first improves team safety and tracking.
Not correct. Delayed reporting increases risk.
Not correct. Team coordination depends on updates.

parts.prompt: What should happen after initial communication?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Table of Contents Table of Contents Introduction Learning Outcomes Icons in This Manual Module One: Introduction to the Sec

choices.text: Set containment and maintain safe positioning.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. Containment limits escape and protects bystanders.

choices.text: Ignore perimeter and focus only on speed.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Not correct. Losing perimeter control raises risk.

choices.text: Complete full notes before scene control.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Not correct. Control the scene first, then document.

name: Manual Scenario, Officer Perspective
aiPoliceChat: Dispatch: Officer approaching suspect in a public zone.
Officer AI Coach: Start with clear verbal commands.
Officer AI Coach: Record actions accurately after control.
imagePrompt: Generate a public transit approach scene with one officer and safe standoff distance.

parts.prompt: What is the best first action?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Table of Contents Table of Contents Introduction Learning Outcomes Icons in This Manual Module One: Introduction to the Sec

choices.text: Use clear verbal commands and assess compliance.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. Start with verbal control before escalation.
Not correct. Force should be lawful, necessary, and proportionate.
Not correct. Clear commands reduce confusion and risk.

parts.prompt: What should happen after control is established?

manual_reference.excerpt: Alberta Solicitor General and Ministry of Public Security Alberta Basic Security Training Jan-14 Table of Contents Table of Contents Introduction Learning Outcomes Icons in This Manual Module One: Introduction to the Sec

choices.text: Document timeline and evidence handoff details.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Correct. Accurate notes support legal and procedural review.

choices.text: Skip documentation and rely on memory.
choices.simplifiedExplanation: Not correct. Delayed notes reduce reliability.
Not correct. Incident details must stay in official records.
